guest_role: Co-founder and CEO at Science Inc (incubator), former CEO at MySpace
assertiveness_why: Prepared questions about ICO mechanics and tokenization with some probing ('Wild West?' 'substitute or complement?'), but mostly receptive. Harry is learning alongside Mike rather than challenging his framing.
question_style: Open-ended questions on ICO mechanics, tokenization, crypto market dynamics; probing questions on whether ICOs feel like the Wild West and whether they substitute or complement venture; mostly learning-oriented; builds on Mike's explanations.
tone: Collaborative and educational; Harry is exploring a new topic; warm but deferential to Mike's crypto expertise; respectful of complexity; engaged but not fawning; makes Bitcoin price prediction bet with Mike.
depth: Very high on crypto/blockchain specifics; covers utility vs. securitized tokens, domain name analogy, ICO mechanics, token sale dynamics, $142B crypto market cap, 11 tokens with >$1B value, M&A complexity, management fee structures, international capital geography (80-90% non-US).
harry_quotes: Why did you first choose this method of financing for the new fund and the incubator?
Do any elements remind you of the Wild West in terms of behavior and concerns?
Is it a substitute? Is it a compliment? How do you view it fundamentally changing venture?
learnings: Utility tokens function like domain name sales but with global liquidity and massive daily volume ($10M-$500M daily ICOs); mechanics are completely different from traditional equity financing.
ICO funding model removes venture capitalists' governance control over founder dilution and equity structuring, creating faster but riskier capital allocation compared to traditional venture.
M&A of tokenized companies creates unresolved legal and structural conflicts between equity holders and token holders that acquirers (Google, Facebook, etc.) have not yet figured out how to handle.
repeat_guest_callback: Mike, it's such a pleasure to have you on the show for round two. What can I say? I so enjoyed the first time that I had to have you back for the very exciting news.
notable: This episode is anchored to a specific news event—Science Inc's ICO presale launching in 13 days. Mike's data ($142B crypto market cap, 11 tokens >$1B, 80-90% international capital) and his M&A unresolved-conflicts observation were cutting-edge for Aug 2017. Harry even makes a Bitcoin price bet ($4,000 this time next year, above/below), showing unusual engagement for him at the topic.
